ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Budae Jjigae (Army Base Stew). Mapped to the broader 'stew (food product)' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor. Budae Jjigae is a Korean fusion stew containing SPAM, sausages, kimchi, instant noodles, baked beans, and cheese.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Soups and stews) is the closest defensible category for Budae Jjigae. No dish-specific FoodEx2 code exists for this Korean fusion stew.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for Budae Jjigae or Army Base Stew as of 2024.

serving.description: 1 bowl with noodles and side dishes
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Army Base Stew is approximately 800 g, served as one large communal or individual bowl containing the stew base with noodles, SPAM, sausages, kimchi, baked beans, and melted cheese, typically accompanied by steamed rice and banchan side dishes.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Soup, beef and vegetable, canned, ready-to-serve (SR Legacy) and component-based estimation for Budae Jjigae
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Budae Jjigae (Army Base Stew) exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values were estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA FoodData Central entries for the primary ingredients: canned luncheon meat (SPAM), cooked pork sausage, kimchi, cooked instant ramen noodles, canned baked beans, processed American cheese, and beef broth. The input record's per_100g values (energy 150 kcal, protein 6.88 g, fat 9.38 g, carbohydrate 10.63 g, sodium 475 mg) are consistent with this component-based estimation and are retained. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, water, and several micronutrients are set to -1 where detailed compositional data for the combined dish is not available in any approved source.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Instant noodles GI analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Budae Jjigae (Army Base Stew). GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being instant ramen noodles (GI ~52–65 per University of Sydney GI Database, mean ~58) combined with baked beans (GI ~40) and gochujang (contains added sugar). The blended dish GI is estimated at approximately 65 given the predominance of refined wheat noodles and the diluting effect of protein and fat from SPAM and sausages. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 65 × carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 85.04 g carbohydrate in 800 g serving) / 100 ≈ 55.3. This is a high glycemic load at the standard 800 g serving size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 475 mg per 100 g; an 800 g serving delivers approximately 3,800 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Army Base Stew due to its very high sodium content from SPAM, processed sausages, gochujang, and broth; if consumed, request reduced SPAM and sausage quantities, use low-sodium broth, and limit portion to no more than 200–300 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined instant noodles (high GI ~65) combined with baked beans and gochujang (added sugar) produce a very high glycemic load of approximately 55 per standard 800 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid the standard serving size of Army Base Stew; if consumed, substitute instant noodles with shirataki or konjac noodles, reduce baked beans, request less gochujang, and limit portion to 200–250 g to manage postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.38 g per 100 g (35.0 g per 800 g serving), trans fat 0.06 g per 100 g, sodium 475 mg per 100 g, and ultra-processed meat components (SPAM, sausages) are all established cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Army Base Stew due to its combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and ultra-processed meat content; if consumed occasionally, reduce SPAM and sausage quantities, substitute with fresh lean protein, and limit portion strictly to 150–200 g.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Ultra-processed pork products (SPAM, sausages) are high in saturated fat and arachidonic acid, amplifying pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways; sodium 475 mg per 100 g worsens RA-related tissue oedema
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute SPAM and processed sausages with tofu or fresh fish, retain kimchi for its probiotic benefit, and reduce gochujang if nightshade sensitivity is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Army Base Stew (Korean: 부대찌개, romanized: Budae Jjigae; Chinese: 部队锅) is a Korean fusion stew that originated in the Uijeongbu and Songtan regions of South Korea after the Korean War (1950–1953), when American military surplus foods such as SPAM, hot dogs, and canned beans became available to the local population. The name 'budae' (부대) means 'military base' and 'jjigae' (찌개) means 'stew'. The dish is prepared by simmering a spicy gochujang and gochugaru-based broth with SPAM, sliced processed sausages or hot dogs, kimchi, instant ramen noodles, canned baked beans, and melted processed cheese in a communal pot. Mushrooms, tofu, onions, garlic, and green onions are common additions. The flavour profile is intensely savoury, spicy, and umami-rich, with a creamy note from the melted cheese. The texture is hearty and thick, with soft noodles, tender meat pieces, and fermented kimchi providing contrast. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 150 kcal, 6.88 g protein, 9.38 g fat (4.38 g saturated), 10.63 g carbohydrate, 1.0 g dietary fibre, and 475 mg sodium. The high sodium content (475 mg per 100 g; ~3,800 mg per standard 800 g serving) makes it a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The refined instant noodle base gives a medium-to-high glycemic index (~65) and a very high glycemic load (~55) per standard serving, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without significant modification. The dish is not halal due to pork-derived SPAM and sausages, and contains gluten, soy, and dairy allergens. It is popular in Korean restaurants in Singapore and across Southeast Asia.

What are the ingredients in Korean army stew?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Budae Jjigae exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from component-weighted averaging of USDA FoodData Central entries for the primary ingredients. The input record's values are consistent with this estimation and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate based on the dominant instant noodle carbohydrate source from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for the combined Budae Jjigae dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for the combined dish is not available in any approved source as of 2024; estimated potassium ~180 mg/100 g was noted in the input record but is not supported by a named approved source and has therefore been set to -1. 3. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the combined dish is not reported in any approved source as of 2024. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: detailed micronutrient data for the combined Budae Jjigae dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as of 2024. 5. The input record listed 'country_of_origin: Singapore' and 'region: Southeast Asia'; however, Budae Jjigae originated in South Korea and is classified as Korean cuisine. The Singapore reference reflects the context of the GrabFood listing, not the dish's origin. 6. The input record's ethnic_tags included 'Bibimbap' and 'Korean Fried Chicken', which are unrelated dishes and have been removed. 7. The dish is not halal due to pork-derived SPAM and processed sausages. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 65 × 85.04 g carbohydrate in 800 g serving) / 100 = 55.3. 9. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule (g4) is not triggered.
